name: data-gov-uk-community-infrastructure-levy5
notes: The Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL) is a charge on new buildings in England and Wales. It is a mechanism to ensure certain types of new development contribute to the infrastructure needed to support that development. This infrastructure will support the growth aspirations for Birmingham as outlined in the Birmingham Development Plan
title: Community Infrastructure Levy

name: data-gov-uk-council-tax-band-d-average
notes: This dataset shows the average Band D council tax amount charged by each billing authority and county council in England. Billing authorities comprise shire districts, unitary authorities, metropolitan districts and London boroughs. The information was collected on the Council Tax Requirement forms submitted by all billing and major precepting authorities t…
publisher_title: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
title: Council Tax Band D Average

name: data-gov-uk-council-tax-chargeable-dwellings
notes: This dataset shows the number of dwellings on the council tax valuation list, compiled by the Valuation Office Agency, which are liable to council tax. This excludes those dwellings which are exempt from council tax or have been demolished. The number of chargeable dwellings shown is a snapshot taken during the September prior to the start of the financial…
publisher_title: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
title: Council Tax Chargeable Dwellings

name: data-gov-uk-council-tax-estimated-collection-rate
notes: The dataset shows the proportion of the council tax net collectable debit which each billing authority expects to be able to collect during the financial year. The estimated collection rate is taken into account when an authority determines the amount of Band D council tax to be charged. The Band D council tax bill is calculated as the council tax requireme…
publisher_title: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
title: Council Tax Estimated Collection Rate

name: data-gov-uk-council-tax-requirement
notes: This dataset shows the total amount of council tax income that each authority has budgeted to use in the forthcoming financial year. It shows the amounts charged to a Band D property, the taxbase, and information on reserves, which the authorities have used to determine the council tax requirement. In calculating this amount for the forthcoming financial ye…
publisher_title: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
title: Council Tax Requirement
